title: Veteran Outpatient Clinics - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Veteran Outpatient Clinics 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS FIND VA LOCATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on VA locations, including VA health, emergency care, VA benefits, and Vet Centers (which offer individual and group counseling). Also provides locations for in-network community care providers, pharmacies, and urgent care for same-day care for minor illnesses or injuries. Locator also includes National Cemeteries. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (866) 620-2071 Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VA OUTPATIENT CLINICS VETERAN OUTPATIENT CLINICS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Outpatient Clinics are part of the VA Portland Health Care System. All locations provide non-emergency primary care, laboratory services, and most sites offer mental health services. Other services vary by location. Veterans receiving care at other VA Medical Centers in the State of Oregon can ask to transfer to another outpatient clinic. Website Eligibility: Veterans; other eligibility requirements may apply Hours: Varies by location Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER
